title: Cohabitation Agreement
description: Agreement for unmarried couples living together, separating individual assets from shared household items.
whatsCovered: Division of lease or mortgage payments
Separate ownership of pre-existing bank balances and assets
Joint bank account setup and monthly contribution targets
Division of household items bought during cohabitation
Waiver of palimony or post-separation financial claims
whenToUse: Use this cohabitation contract before moving in with a partner to define how rent, bills, and property divisions are handled if the relationship ends.

fields.label: Household Expense Split (Party A% / Party B%)

clauses.heading: COHABITATION AGREEMENT
clauses.body: This agreement or policy (the "Agreement") is executed as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {party_a_name}, residing/located at {party_a_address} ("First Party"), and {party_b_name}, residing/located at {party_b_address} ("Second Party").

clauses.heading: 1. Core Framework
clauses.body: This agreement establishes the legal rights, bylaws, transfers, waivers, or consent structures for the Cohabitation Agreement. The parties agree to adhere to the terms and declarations set out below.

clauses.heading: 2. Shared Household Expenses
clauses.body: Unmarried partners agree to share rent and food costs matching split: {joint_expense_split}. Pre-existing assets remain separate property.

clauses.heading: General Legal Terms
clauses.body: This document constitutes the entire understanding between the parties. Any amendments must be made in writing and executed by both parties.

clauses.heading: Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: Can I customize this legal template?
faq.answer: Yes, you can edit this template using the online editor, and add custom schedules or exhibits.

faq.question: Is this agreement binding?
faq.answer: Yes, once signed by all parties, it becomes a legally binding contract under state laws.
